title: Form 14 — Notice of Motion
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 14)
helpIntro: A Notice of Motion tells the court and the other party that you want to ask for a temporary order — for example, temporary custody, support, or a restraining order — before your main case is decided. You must serve this form on the other party at least 6 business days before the motion date.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 6
parts.intro: We'll use your court information from your existing case file. Confirm or update the details below.

fields.label: Which courthouse will hear this motion?
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice
fields.helpText: This should be the same courthouse as your main case.

fields.label: Court file number
fields.helpText: Found on any documents already filed in your case. Leave blank if you don't have one yet.

fields.label: Date of the motion hearing
fields.helpText: Contact the court clerk to schedule a date before filling this in. You must serve this form at least 6 business days before this date.

fields.label: Place of the hearing (courthouse address)
fields.placeholder: e.g. 393 University Ave, Toronto ON M5G 1E6
fields.helpText: Usually the same as the courthouse above. Your clerk can confirm the exact courtroom.

parts.title: The parties
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 6
parts.intro: Confirm your information and the other party's information. This auto-fills from your case profile.

fields.label: Your full legal name (person making this motion)
Your address for service
fields.helpText: This is the address where court documents can be delivered to you.

fields.label: Does the other party have a lawyer?

parts.title: Orders you are asking for
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 6
parts.intro: Select all the temporary orders you want the court to make at this motion. These are temporary — they last until your case is resolved or the court changes them.

fields.label: Exclusive possession of the family home
Change or set aside an existing order
Describe the other order you are asking for
fields.placeholder: Describe clearly what you want the court to order.

fields.label: In your own words, describe the specific orders you are asking for (Page 2 of Form 14)
fields.placeholder:
e.g. 1. That the Applicant have temporary decision-making responsibility for the child Alex Lance, born April 12, 2015.
2. That the Respondent pay child support of $X per month commencing [date].
3. That the Respondent be restrained from attending at [address].
fields.helpText: Write each order you want as a numbered sentence. Be specific — include names, amounts, and dates where possible. A judge will read exactly what you write here.

parts.title: Supporting evidence
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 6
parts.intro: Tell the court what documents you are attaching to support this motion.

fields.label: Are you filing a Form 14A Affidavit with this motion?
fields.helpText: Almost always yes. The affidavit is where you explain the facts behind your motion in detail.

fields.label: Are you also serving a Notice of Case Conference with this motion?
fields.helpText: Required if you are asking to change an existing order.

fields.label: List any other documents in the Continuing Record you are relying on
fields.placeholder:
e.g. Volume 1, Tab 3 — Financial Statement dated January 2024
Volume 1, Tab 5 — Prior Order dated March 2023
fields.helpText: Leave blank if this is your first court document. The Continuing Record is the binder of all documents filed in your case.

fields.label: Is this an urgent motion? If yes, briefly explain why.
fields.placeholder: e.g. The child is at risk of harm. I am asking for an emergency order without notice because...
fields.helpText: Only fill this in if you are asking the court to hear this motion on an urgent basis or without notifying the other party first (ex parte).

parts.title: Serving the other party
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 6
parts.intro: You must give the other party a copy of this Notice of Motion and all supporting documents at least 6 business days before the motion date. After you serve them you must fill out Form 6B (Affidavit of Service).

fields.label: How will you serve the other party?
options: By email (if they have agreed or if the court has ordered it)
fields.helpText: If the other party has a lawyer, serve the lawyer — not the person directly.

fields.label: When do you plan to serve the other party?
fields.helpText: Must be at least 6 business days before your motion date.

fields.label: I understand I must file Form 14C (Confirmation of Motion) no later than 2:00 p.m., 3 business days before the motion date.
fields.helpText: If you do not file Form 14C on time, your motion may be removed from the list and you will have to reschedule.

parts.title: Review and sign
parts.subtitle: Step 6 of 6
parts.intro: Review your Notice of Motion below. When you are ready, confirm and generate your form.

fields.label: Date you are signing this form
I confirm the information in this Notice of Motion is accurate. I understand this document will be filed with the Ontario court.
